and sale of these parts, which is done in its factories, profits are being 
generated. These profits, for our purposes and for the purpose of the 
Payment of Bonus Act, 1965 (hereinafter ‘Bonus Act’), are known 
as ‘surplus’. Thus, though the factory may be under the control of a 
trust, but it is also governed under the Factories Act, 1948. 
4. 
It is again an admitted fact that the workmen who are employed in 
the factories in various capacities largely comprise of such workers 
who have been cured of Leprosy or are otherwise differently abled. 
These workmen have a union known as “WORTH Trust Workers 
Union” (hereinafter referred to as ‘Union’). In the year 1998, this 
Union raised an industrial dispute demanding bonus and ex-gratia 
for the year 1996-97 and ultimately, the dispute was referred to the 
[2025] 5 S.C.R.  
363
The Management of Worth Trust v. 
The Secretary, Worth Trust Workers Union
